sourceTitle: How tech startups build their company operating systems in Notion

questions.question: What is the recommended first step for a busy founder who wants to create a Company OS in Notion?

options.text: Start from a blank page and build the wiki manually
options.explanation: While possible, the guide suggests using a template for efficiency.

options.text: Duplicate the 'Company Home' template from the Notion template library
options.explanation: The guide advises busy founders to start by duplicating the pre‑made Company Home template.

options.text: Import an existing Confluence wiki directly
options.explanation: Importing is an option later, but not the first recommended step.

options.text: Create a new teamspace and invite all employees
options.explanation: Creating a teamspace is useful, but the guide emphasizes starting with the template.

questions.question: Which Notion feature allows you to view a database inside a wiki page without leaving the page?

options.text: Linked database view
options.explanation: You can embed an inline or linked view of any database directly in a wiki page.

options.text: Page export as CSV
options.explanation: Exporting creates a file; it does not embed the live database.

options.text: Inline code block
options.explanation: Code blocks display code, not database content.

options.text: Sidebar shortcut
options.explanation: The sidebar navigates pages but does not embed a database view.

questions.question: How can you quickly return to the top‑level Wiki home page when you are deep inside nested sub‑pages?

options.text: Click the breadcrumb at the top left of the sub‑page
options.explanation: The breadcrumb provides a fast way back to the parent page or home.

options.text: Use the browser back button
options.explanation: The back button may not always lead to the Wiki home, especially after navigating elsewhere.

options.text: Search for 'Company Home' in the search bar
options.explanation: Search works but is slower than using the breadcrumb.

options.text: Refresh the page
options.explanation: Refreshing reloads the current page; it does not navigate to the home.

questions.question: Which property would you use to assign responsibility for keeping a wiki page up to date?

options.text: Created time
options.explanation: Created time records when the page was made, not who maintains it.

options.text: Owner
options.explanation: The Owner property is designed to tag the team member responsible for the page.

options.text: Last edited by
options.explanation: Last edited by shows who last changed the page, not who is assigned to maintain it.

options.text: Tags
options.explanation: Tags can categorize content but do not designate a responsible person.

questions.question: What advantage does embedding a database view of meeting notes in the wiki provide?

options.text: It automatically deletes old meeting notes after a week
options.explanation: Embedding does not delete content; it merely displays it.

options.text: Team members can see the most recent meetings without navigating away from the wiki
options.explanation: Embedding lets users view and sort recent notes directly on the wiki page.

options.text: It converts meeting notes into a PDF for download
options.explanation: Embedding does not change file format.

options.text: It locks the meeting notes so no one can edit them
options.explanation: Embedding does not affect edit permissions.

questions.question: If you want to prevent accidental edits to a benefits policy page, which Notion feature should you adjust?

options.text: Page icon
options.explanation: Icons are visual only and do not affect permissions.

options.text: Page cover
options.explanation: Covers are decorative; they do not control editing.

options.text: Permission settings in the Share menu
options.explanation: Adjusting permissions (e.g., view‑only) stops unauthorized edits.

options.text: Adding a comment at the top of the page
options.explanation: Comments are for discussion, not for restricting edits.

questions.question: Which of the following best describes how Notion AI can assist with Company OS goals?

options.text: It automatically updates the status of every goal based on external data sources
options.explanation: Notion AI provides suggestions and summaries but does not autonomously change data.

options.text: It can summarize progress on recorded goals and suggest next steps
options.explanation: The guide states AI can summarize goal progress and offer recommendations.

options.text: It replaces the need for a human manager to review goal metrics
options.explanation: AI assists, but human oversight remains necessary.

options.text: It creates new company goals based on trending industry topics
options.explanation: AI does not auto‑generate goals without user input.

questions.question: When integrating Slack with Notion, what primary benefit does the integration provide?

options.text: It allows you to edit Notion pages directly from Slack messages
options.explanation: Slack integration notifies, but editing still occurs in Notion.

options.text: Team members receive notifications in Slack about important changes made in Notion
options.explanation: The guide highlights that Slack integration keeps members informed of changes.

options.text: It syncs all Notion databases as Slack channels
options.explanation: Databases are not turned into channels; they remain in Notion.

options.text: It automatically backs up Notion pages to a Slack archive
options.explanation: Backup is not mentioned as a Slack integration feature.

questions.question: What is the effect of treating the Company Home page as a database entry?

options.text: The page can no longer contain text blocks or embeds
options.explanation: The guide notes you can still mix text, embeds, etc., with the database entry.

options.text: You can tag the page with properties like Owner, Created time, and Last edited time
options.explanation: Treating it as a database entry enables adding metadata properties.

options.text: The page becomes read‑only for all team members
options.explanation: Database status does not enforce read‑only automatically.

options.text: It forces the page to display only in table view
options.explanation: You can still choose any view; the database entry does not lock the view type.

questions.question: Which step is NOT part of customizing a page’s visual appearance according to the guide?

options.text: Clicking 'Add icon' at the top of the page
options.explanation: Adding an icon is a recommended visual customization.

options.text: Choosing a page cover image
options.explanation: Choosing a cover is also suggested for visual appeal.

options.text: Changing the page’s font style from the sidebar
options.explanation: The guide does not mention changing font style via the sidebar; fonts are set globally, not per page.

options.text: Using clear fonts and drag‑and‑drop editing for aesthetics
options.explanation: The guide highlights Notion’s clear fonts as part of the aesthetic.
